title: S.R. Bommai v. Union of India
summary: The Supreme Court ruled that imposition of President's Rule under Article 356 is subject to judicial review. The court held secularism and federalism are essential features of the basic structure of the Constitution. It restrained the arbitrary misuse of Article 356 by requiring the President's satisfaction to be based on relevant material.
key_holdings: Imposition of President's Rule under Article 356 is subject to judicial review
Secularism is a basic feature of the Constitution
Federalism is part of the basic structure
Floor test must be held before recommending President's Rule
Court can restore dismissed government if imposition was unconstitutional
key_quotes: Secularism is the basic feature of the Constitution.
The floor of the House is the place to test majority.
why_important: Drastically curtailed arbitrary use of Article 356 by the Union government against state governments. Firmly established secularism and federalism as basic structure elements.
relevance_to_legal_finance_system: Relevant to centre-state financial relations and GST council disputes under cooperative federalism.
